methods.description: This method describes the process of preparing ice core samples for tephra analysis to determine their relation to volcanic eruptions, specifically the V1477 eruption.

steps.description: Archived ice from the interval between 78.515-78.655 m was sampled directly for tephra analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: sample ice core sections for tephra analysis

steps.description: The direct ice samples were transferred to Nalgene bottles, melted at room temperature and the meltwater centrifuged to concentrate any particulate material present.
steps.searchable_summary: melt ice samples and centrifuge to concentrate particulates

steps.description: The concentrated meltwater was evaporated onto a frosted microprobe slide and covered with Buehler EpoxyCure 2 resin.
steps.searchable_summary: evaporate meltwater onto slide and cover with resin

steps.description: The presence of glass tephra shards was assessed at 100x-400x magnification using a polarising light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: assess glass tephra shards using polarising light microscope

steps.description: To section the glass shards and create a flat surface for geochemical analysis, the slide was ground using 12 µm alumina powder then polished using 6, 3 and 1 µm diamond paste.
steps.searchable_summary: grind and polish slide for geochemical analysis

steps.description: The surface of the resin and exposed shards was then carbon coated prior to the oxide concentrations of 11 major and minor elements within individual glass shards being analysed using a JEOL FEG SEM 6500F.
steps.searchable_summary: carbon coat slide and analyze oxide concentrations using SEM

steps.description: Glass from the rhyolitic Lipari obsidian and the basaltic Laki 1783 CE tephra were analysed as secondary standards during the analytical period and compared to recommended values.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze secondary standards and compare to recommended values

methods.method_name: Geochemical Data Normalization and Statistical Analysis
methods.description: This method involves normalizing geochemical data to an anhydrous basis and performing statistical comparisons to analyze tephra occurrences.

steps.description: Geochemical data were normalised to an anhydrous basis using eight elements consistently analysed in all studies.
steps.searchable_summary: normalize geochemical data to anhydrous basis

steps.description: Statistical comparisons were made between tephra occurrences using the statistical distance (D2) test and the similarity coefficient (SC) function.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical comparisons using D2 test and SC function
